ucs.title: [LLM] HEXANE/Lyceum password-spray pivot to RDP against ME O&G/telecom tenants
ucs.description: Hunts HEXANE's signature low-and-slow password-spray campaigns (DanBot/Shark operators are well-documented spraying telecom and oil & gas tenants in Israel, Saudi, Kuwait, Tunisia, Morocco) followed within hours by an interactive RDP/network logon from the same successful account — the classic Lyceum 'spray-then-RDP' chain that distinguishes them from APT33/OilRig.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstFail max(_time) as lastFail dc(Authentication.user) as failed_user_count values(Authentication.user) as sprayed_users from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="failure" Authentication.app IN ("azuread","o365","adfs") by Authentication.src _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | where failed_user_count >= 15 | join type=inner src [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as success_time values(Authentication.user) as success_user from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" Authentication.app IN ("azuread","o365","adfs") by Authentication.src Authentication.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`] | where success_time >= firstFail AND success_time <= (lastFail + 7200) | join type=inner success_user [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as rdp_time values(Authentication.dest) as pivot_host from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.authentication_method="RemoteInteractive" OR Authentication.signature="4624" Authentication.logon_type IN (10,3) by Authentication.user Authentication.src | rename Authentication.user as success_user | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`] | where rdp_time >= success_time AND rdp_time <= (success_time + 21600) | table firstFail src failed_user_count success_user success_time rdp_time pivot_host
ucs.rationale: Lyceum/HEXANE has been repeatedly burned (SecureWorks, ClearSky, Kaspersky Siamesekitten reports) running large-volume password sprays against externally-exposed OWA/Azure AD tenants in MENA telecom and energy verticals, then immediately reusing the cracked credential for hands-on-keyboard RDP rather than the longer-dwell C2 deployments seen with APT33/OilRig. ucs.title: [LLM] HEXANE DanBot/Shark loader: Office macro spawning WMI Event Subscription persistence
ucs.description: Detects the HEXANE post-exploitation chain where a maldoc lure (frequently masquerading as O&G or HR/recruiter content per the Siamesekitten campaigns) executes a VBA macro that drops Shark/Milan/DanBot and immediately registers a WMI __EventFilter+CommandLineEventConsumer for persistence — a combo Lyceum has used since 2018 and that is rare in benign Office workflows.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as office_spawn_time values(Processes.process) as child_cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as wmi_time values(Processes.process) as wmi_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*subscription*","*__EventFilter*","*CommandLineEventConsumer*","*ActiveScriptEventConsumer*")) OR (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" AND (Processes.process="*Set-WmiInstance*" OR Processes.process="*Register-WmiEvent*" OR Processes.process="*__EventFilter*" OR Processes.process="*CommandLineEventConsumer*")) by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where wmi_time >= office_spawn_time AND wmi_time <= (office_spawn_time + 1800) | table office_spawn_time dest user parent_process_name child_proc child_cmdline wmi_time wmi_cmdline
ucs.kql:
let LureExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe")
    | project LureTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, Lure=InitiatingProcessFileName, Child=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let WmiPersist =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("subscription","__EventFilter","CommandLineEventConsumer","ActiveScriptEventConsumer"))
         or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Set-WmiInstance","Register-WmiEvent","__EventFilter","CommandLineEventConsumer","ActiveScriptEventConsumer","root\\subscription"))
    | project WmiTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, WmiProc=FileName, WmiCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
LureExec
| join kind=inner WmiPersist on DeviceId
| where WmiTime between (LureTime .. LureTime + 30m)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemotePort in (53, 443, 80)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","outlook.exe")
    | summarize Beacons=count(), DistinctRemotes=dcount(RemoteIP), SampleHosts=make_set(RemoteUrl, 10) by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
) on DeviceId
| project LureTime, DeviceName, AccountName, Lure, Child, ChildCmd, WmiTime, WmiProc, WmiCmd, Beacons, DistinctRemotes, SampleHosts
| order by LureTime desc
ucs.rationale: WMI Event Subscription persistence is a HEXANE staple documented across DanBot, Shark, Milan and the Siamesekitten campaign (SecureWorks 2019, Kaspersky 2021, ClearSky). Pairing it with the immediate parent being an Office app — driven by the actor's recruiter/HR-themed lures aimed at Israeli, Saudi, and Tunisian targets — produces a chain that is essentially unique to Lyceum's loaders within MENA
